title: Purified human antibody binding the microbial heparin binding motif and method of using
abstract: A purified human antibody to peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1) and a method for purifying same is provided. Methods and reagents for ameliorating or preventing biofilm formation on a surface of an indwelling or implanted device in a patient resulting in decreased virulence of microorganisms such as Candida species and/or Staphylococcus species by administering the purified human antibody to peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1) are also provided.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1 . A human antibody to peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1 ) obtained by purifying the human antibody from a commercially available human antibody composition resulting in the purified human antibody. 2. The human antibody of claim 1 obtained by purifying the commercially available GAMMAGARDÂ® human antibody composition. 3. The human antibody of claim 1 where affinity purifying comprises contacting the composition with a stationary phase comprising a support, the support containing an affinity ligand consisting of HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1), and eluting the purified human antibody from the stationary phase. 4. The human antibody of claim 3 further comprising collecting the eluted purified human antibody and optionally dialyzing the collected human antibody. 5. The human antibody of claim 3 further comprising cleaving the affinity purified antibody to produce a Fab fragment of the antibody. 6. The human antibody of claim 5 where cleaving is by papain treatment. 7. A purified human antibody to peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1 ), the human antibody purified by a method comprising contacting a composition comprising the human antibody with a stationary phase, the stationary phase consisting of a solid support and an affinity ligand, the affinity ligand consisting of the peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1), washing the stationary phase with a wash buffer, and eluting the human antibody from the stationary phase by contacting an elution buffer with the stationary phase. 8. The human antibody of claim 7, wherein the composition comprising the human antibody is a commercially available GAMMAGARDÂ® human antibody composition. 9. The human antibody of claim 7, wherein the composition comprising the human antibody is human plasma. 10. The purified human antibody of claim 7 further comprising cleaving the affinity purified antibody to produce an Fab fragment of the antibody. 1 1 . A method to reduce or prevent biofilm on a surface of an indwelling or implanted medical device in a patient, the method comprising administering before, during, and/or after installation or implantation of a medical device in the patient a composition comprising at least one biocompatible excipient and a purified human antibody to a peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1), under conditions to result in reduced or prevented biofilm formation on the implanted or indwelling medical device. 12. The method of claim 1 1 where the biofilm is from at least one of Enterococcus faecalis (Â£. faecalis), Staphylococcus aureus (St. aureus), Enterobacter cloacae (Â£. cloacae), or Acinetobacter baumanii (A. baumanii), and where the purified human antibody to peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1 ) is purified by a method comprising contacting a composition comprising the human antibody with a stationary phase, the stationary phase consisting of a solid support and an affinity ligand, the affinity ligand consisting of the peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1), washing the stationary phase with a wash buffer, and eluting the human antibody from the stationary phase by contacting an elution buffer with the stationary phase. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the composition comprising the human antibody is a commercially available GAMMAGARDÂ® human antibody composition. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the composition comprising the human antibody is human plasma. 15. The method of claim 1 1 where the implanted device is selected from the group consisting of a venous catheter, an arterial catheter, a central line, a hemodialysis catheter, a peritoneal catheter, a peripherally inserted central catheter, a urinary tract catheter, a central nervous system shunt, a peritoneal dialysis catheter, a dialysis shunt, and combinations thereof. 16. The method of claim 11 resulting in decreased patient infection. 17. The method of claim 1 1 resulting in decreased microorganism virulence. 18. The method of claim 1 1 further comprising cleaving the purified human antibody to produce an Fab fragment of the antibody, and using the Fab fragment, precluding immune activation by the Fc portion of the antibody and counterproductive binding. 19. An Fab fragment of a human antibody to peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1) obtained by purifying the human antibody from a commercially available human antibody composition, and cleaving the human antibody resulting in the Fab fragment of the human antibody. 20. The Fab fragment of claim 19 where cleaving is by papain treatment. 21 . A method to reduce or prevent biofilm on a surface of an indwelling or implanted medical device in a patient, the method comprising administering before, during, and/or after installation or implantation of a medical device in the patient a composition comprising at least one biocompatible excipient and an Fab fragment of a purified human antibody to a peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1), under conditions to result in reduced or prevented biofilm formation on the implanted or indwelling medical device. 22. The method of claim 21 where the biofilm is from at least one of Enterococcus faecalis (Â£. faecalis), Staphylococcus aureus (St. aureus), Enterobacter cloacae (Â£. cloacae), or Acinetobacter baumanii (A. baumanii), and where the Fab fragment of the purified human antibody to peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1) is obtained by a method comprising contacting a composition comprising the human antibody with a stationary phase, the stationary phase consisting of a solid support and an affinity ligand consisting of the peptide HKQEKQKKHQIHKV (SEQ ID NO: 1 ), washing the stationary phase with a wash buffer, eluting the human antibody from the stationary phase by contacting an elution buffer with the stationary phase, treating the eluate with an enzyme that cleaves the human antibody, contacting the treated eluate with a protein A stationary phase, and collecting the Fab fragments of the human antibody. 23. The method of claim 22 where the enzyme that cleaves the human antibody is papain. 24. The method of claim 22, wherein the composition comprising the human antibody is a commercially available GAMMAGARDÂ® human antibody composition. 25. The method of claim 22, wherein the composition comprising the human antibody is human plasma.
